title: THE MIDNIGHT BUS
subtitle: Vol 5 · Ch VII · The Chariot
notes: Cora's bus on the late-shift route between Graustark and the next town up the river. The bus is the location. The route is the doom-clock. Twelve spaces — driver's seat at the front, rows back through the aisle, the door, the standee bar, the overhead, the radio. The bus is always moving (unless you PULL OVER); the landscape outside changes every turn.
ambient_pool: the wipers move once and stop. cora doesn't remember turning them on.
the road sounds shift — pavement to gravel to pavement.
the dashboard radio is tuned between two stations.
the back rows are darker than they should be at this hour.
the headlights catch a deer that doesn't move.
the door's pneumatics hiss without being asked.

spaces.label: NEXT STOP
spaces.flavor: the scheduled stop ahead — wherever the route's next disembarkation is.
arrival_lines: the next stop is in sight through the windshield.
the pull-cord by the door has been pulled twice without anyone touching it.

spaces.label: SHOULDER
spaces.flavor: the highway shoulder · pull over here for unscheduled stops or to leap.
arrival_lines: the shoulder is wide enough for the bus and not much else.
the gravel under the wheels is loud in a way the pavement isn't.

spaces.label: DESTINATION
spaces.flavor: the end of the route · the bus depot in the next town.
arrival_lines: the destination depot is lit even at this hour.
the parking-brake handle is in your hand. you don't remember reaching for it.

spaces.label: DRIVER SEAT
spaces.flavor: cora's seat · steering wheel, gear shift, the small fan, the mirror.
arrival_lines: the seat has the shape of cora. it has had it since 1991.
the mirror is angled to see the whole aisle.

spaces.label: FRONT DOOR
spaces.flavor: the bus door · pneumatics, fare box, the step-down.
arrival_lines: the door's pneumatics hiss when you stand next to them.
the fare box has tonight's quarters in it. they are warm.

spaces.label: AISLE · FRONT
spaces.flavor: the front rows · the standees, the people who don't sit.
arrival_lines: the front rows have someone in every other seat.
the standee bar is at chest height. cold.

spaces.label: AISLE · MIDDLE
spaces.flavor: the middle rows · the sit-down passengers · the longest stretch.
arrival_lines: the middle of the bus has a sound the front and back don't.
two seats facing each other in the middle — passengers can talk if they want to.

spaces.label: BACK ROW
spaces.flavor: the back of the bus · the wide row · the kids and the late-shift workers.
arrival_lines: the back row is darker. the dome light is out.
someone is sleeping. you can hear them.

spaces.label: OVERHEAD
spaces.flavor: the overhead racks · luggage, dropped scarves, things that fell forward.
arrival_lines: the overhead has a single duffel bag in it.
something rolled forward when you braked.

spaces.label: DASHBOARD
spaces.flavor: the dashboard · the radio, the route-book, the small notes cora keeps.
arrival_lines: the radio is between two stations.
the route-book is open to tonight's run.

spaces.label: PASSENGER RADIO
spaces.flavor: the small CB radio cora keeps for talking to other drivers.
arrival_lines: the CB is on. channel 17.
someone on the channel is asking about the weather.

spaces.label: UNDER SEAT
spaces.flavor: the space under the seats · where dropped things end up.
arrival_lines: a glove. a small notebook. a coin from a country you don't know.
something has been here for a long time. you keep meaning to pick it up.

miles_track.label: MILES
miles_track.notes: Cora's positive doom-clock. DRIVE ticks miles up. At threshold the bus has arrived at the destination — the win condition becomes available. Some scenarios cap maximum miles to force you to manage the route carefully.

ladder.label: the depot. headlights on, parking brake off.
first mile. the city is still in the windshield.
the river bridge. the bus shifts under you.
halfway. the small farm with the floodlight.
the long stretch · trees, no buildings.
destination · the depot lights of the next town.
